author: Jason Gross
Jason Gross
message_subject: Split FMapTrie proofs and definitions for positivity checker (#1273)
message_body:
* Split FMapTrie proofs and definitions for positivity checker

* Split up the shape module

* Fix trie shape ind so that it's actually true

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
It compiles, but unfortunately the positivity checker is unhappy with
our schema for non-empty maps :-(, see
https://github.com/coq/coq/issues/16120

It's also very slow :-(
```
src/Util/FSets/FMapTrie.vo (real: 194.55, user: 193.16, sys: 1.24, mem: 818488 ko)
```

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
I've left some unused tactics in there because they may be useful
references in the future, or as a prototype for tries.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Organize and fill out structures and orders (#1234)
message_body:
We're nearly at the point where we can use something like `FMapAVL` to
optimize `Assembly/Symbolic`.  (Tries would be better, but also harder.)

What remains:
1. `src/Util/FSets/FMapSect` for showing that we don't need a full
   isomorphism to port FMap structure, only an injection
2. `src/Util/FSets/FMapOption`
3. `src/Util/FSets/FMapSum`
4. `src/Util/FSets/FMapFlip` (maybe?)
5. `src/Util/FSets/FMapProd`
6. `src/Util/FSets/FMapListViaAVL` (or similar)
7. Describing the injection from `op` to something like `N * option
   AccessSize * option symbol * option N * option N`.
8. Replacing dag with mapping from `idx` to `node idx * description`,
   and mapping from `node idx` to `idx`, and an `N` holding the total
   size of the dag.
9. Updating corresponding proofs.
10. (optional) using tries instead of AVL for list-keys

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add a bunch of iso-based FMap and MSet stuff (#1222)

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Split off the non-bedrock-dependent part of equiv proofs
message_body:
This will hopefully make development a bit faster; these files are so
long.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add some lemmas about Forall2 and concat

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
All the lemmas in Coq.Lists.List and Coq.Sorting.Permutation present in
current master (8.16?) but not in 8.11.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add various utility lemmas and definitions (#1100)
message_body:
* Add various utility lemmas and definitions

Including `inversion_list`, `inversion_pair`, `inversion_nat_rel`,
`inversion_nat_eq`.

* Fix for Coq 8.12

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Fix for older versions of Coq

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This, combined with MSets.Iso, will let us make an MSet based on
MSetPositive for int.type in the stringification machinery.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add more util definitions and lemmas

author: jadep
message_subject: fixed some subtleties in the argument-loading logic

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
Some proofs were changed because I originally switched `Qed` to
`Defined`, and changed proofs to fit that, and didn't bother changing
the proofs back when I decided `Defined` wasn't needed.

The primary thing this commit adds is an improvement to the
`reflect_beq_to_eq` tactic.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Make [reflect] a typeclass and add a bunch of lemmas

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This closes #146 and makes `make quick` faster.

The changes were generated by adding [Global Set Suggest Proof Using.]
to GlobalSettings.v, and then following [the instructions for a script I
wrote](https://github.com/JasonGross/coq-tools#proof-using-helper).

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add decidable instances for sumwise and fieldwise

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This will be useful for the Weierstrass curves, which require
case-splitting on field equality.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Do some work pair-programming with Andres on opts
message_body: Partially pre-compile various optimizations in GF25519.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add coqprime that works with 8.5, bundle bedrock
message_body:
This simplifes the build process, and also allows us to try to build
with 8.5.  We autodetect the version of Coq in the Makefile to decide
which version of coqprime to build.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Do some work pair-programming with Andres on opts
message_body: Partially pre-compile various optimizations in GF25519.
